LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-23-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE VALENCIA DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED "IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE" IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 20, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. The Valencia declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 435.5. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the complaint is now due by February 20, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
